results.rendered_html: yamunā f. N. of a river commonly called the Jumnā (in  Hariv.  &  MārkP.  identified with Yamī q.v.; it rises in the Himālaya mountains among the Jumnotri peaks at an elevation of 10,849 feet, and flows for 860 miles before it joins the Ganges at Allahabad, its water being there clear as crystal, while that of the Ganges is yellowish; the confluence of the two with the river Sarasvatī, supposed to join them underground, is called tri-veṇī, q.v.),  RV.  &c. &c.

results.rendered_html:
yamunā
1 N. of a celebrated river (regarded as a sister of Yama).
2 N. of Durgā.
Comp.
— patiḥ N. of Viṣṇu.
— bhid m. N. of Balarāma.
— bhrātṛ m. Yama, the god of death.
